key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
  "name": "smartresponsor/accessing",
  "description": "Symfony 8 account access application for registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, and security events.",
  "type": "project",
  "license": "proprietary",
  "require": {
    "php": "^8.4",
    "ext-ctype": "*",
    "ext-iconv": "*",
    "doctrine/doctrine-bundle": "3.1",
    "doctrine/doctrine-migrations-bundle": "3.6",
    "doctrine/orm": "^3.3",
    "scheb/2fa-bundle": "8.5",
    "scheb/2fa-totp": "8.5",
    "symfony/console": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/dotenv": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/form": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/framework-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/mailer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/monolog-bundle": "^3.10",
    "symfony/notifier": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/password-hasher": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/rate-limiter": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/runtime": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-csrf": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/serializer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/twig-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/translation": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/validator": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/yaml": "8.0.*",
    "symfonycasts/reset-password-bundle": "^1.23",
    "symfonycasts/verify-email-bundle": "^1.17",
    "twig/extra-bundle": "^3.15",
    "twig/twig": "^3.15"
  },
  "require-dev": {
    "doctrine/doctrine-fixtures-bundle": "^4.1",
    "fakerphp/faker": "^1.24",
    "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "3.94",
    "phpmd/phpmd": "^2.15",
    "phpstan/phpstan": "^2.1",
    "symfony/browser-kit": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/css-selector": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/maker-bundle": "^1.64",
    "symfony/panther": "^2.2",
    "symfony/phpunit-bridge": "^7.3"
  },
  "autoload": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\": "src/"
    }
  },
  "autoload-dev": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\Tests\\": "tests/"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "php -l $(git ls-files '*.php')",
    "cs:check": "php-cs-fixer fix --dry-run --diff --verbose",
    "cs:fix": "php-cs-fixer fix --verbose",
    "stan": "phpstan analyse",
    "test": "vendor/bin/simple-phpunit",
    "pipeline:local:full": [
      "@lint",
      "@cs:check",
      "@stan",
      "@test"
    ]
  },
  "extra": {
    "public-dir": "public",
    "product-name": "Accessing",
    "core-entity": "Account"
  },
  "minimum-stability": "stable",
  "prefer-stable": true,
  "config": {
    "allow-plugins": {
      "symfony/runtime": true
    }
  }
}

verdict.summary: Symfony 8 access-management component with clear product identity, solid architecture, and broad test layers; main RC blockers are missing CI/ops automation and docs publication.
strengths: Comprehensive test layout (unit, integration, functional, browser, e2e)
Built-in security features (2FA, email verification, reset, rate limiting)
Composer scripts for lint, CS, static analysis, and tests
risks: Static analysis and PHPUnit configs only as .dist
Docs not wired to Antora report path
Proprietary license may limit external adoption
next_actions: Add GitHub Actions to run lint, cs:check, stan, unit/integration/functional, and Playwright e2e on PRs
Promote phpstan.neon and phpunit.xml from .dist with tuned rules and coverage thresholds
Publish product/architecture docs into .antora-src and wire report index
Document and add health/metrics endpoints and basic runtime checks
Define release and migration steps using existing Doctrine migrations
notes: Assessment grounded in repository tree and manifests at commit bb0013dfbd4df3f201d4cae167f5de4bdf58967f
Composer requires PHP ^8.4; ensure runtime environments match this requirement

product.summary: Product identity is explicit for account access (registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, security events) with supporting manifests.
risks: Documentation not integrated with the Antora report path
improvements: Publish product overview and user flows into .antora-src and link the report index

architecture.summary: Symfony 8 with Doctrine ORM 3 and layered structure (Entity, RepositoryInterface, ServiceInterface) plus recent migrations indicate a consistent architecture.
risks: Static analysis is configured only via phpstan.neon.dist, reducing enforced consistency
improvements: Commit a project-level phpstan.neon with agreed rule levels and baselines

runtime.summary: Runtime surface is solid via Symfony Runtime, Monolog, and RateLimiter with environment configs and a standard public entrypoint.
risks: No explicit metrics/tracing or performance instrumentation observed
improvements: Add health and metrics endpoints and document operational runbooks

qa.summary: Broad QA posture with unit, integration, functional, Panther browser, and Playwright e2e tests plus lint/CS/static analysis scripts.
risks: No CI present to enforce test and static analysis gates
phpunit.xml and phpstan config are only provided as .dist
improvements: Introduce CI to run pipeline:local:full and npm run test:e2e
Promote and tune phpunit.xml and phpstan.neon with coverage thresholds and rule levels

ops.summary: Strong in-app security posture (2FA, verify email, reset password, password hashing, rate limiting) and logging via Monolog; delivery/ops automation is not in-repo.
risks: No GitHub workflows or deployment automation found
Secrets management approach not documented beyond Dotenv for dev
improvements: Add CI/CD workflows leveraging existing composer scripts and migrations
Document runtime observability and integrate metrics/tracing
Define environment-specific configuration and secret handling guidance

market.summary: Market alignment is clear for an access-management component, with manifests and README describing scope and positioning; license is proprietary.
risks: Proprietary license may limit external contribution and adoption
improvements: Surface integration scenarios and positioning in published documentation

overall.summary: Engineering shape and feature set appear RC-ready, but missing CI/ops automation and documentation publication keep the component pre-RC.
risks: Lack of CI/CD and observability slows RC confidence
improvements: Implement CI pipelines and publish Antora documentation to close RC gaps
